args.message: Great question — and I'm happy to tell you this one's got a clean history. No accidents, no damage reports, nothing. It's exactly the kind of car you want for a daily commute: reliable, efficient, and no surprises. Toyota hybrids are known for going 200,000+ miles without breaking a sweat, and at 71,000, this one's barely broken in. Have you driven a Prius before? The fuel savings alone will have this car paying for itself in no time.

text: That's good to hear. What kind of maintenance records do you have for it?

speaker: seller

args.message: Oh, this one was babied. Previous owner was meticulous — we've got records showing regular oil changes, tire rotations, and the hybrid system was just inspected about 3,000 miles ago. Everything checked out perfectly. Toyota recommends their hybrid battery health check around 60-70k, and this one passed with flying colors. Brakes still have plenty of life, fluids are fresh — honestly, it's about as turnkey as a used car gets. You could drive this off the lot today and not worry about a thing for the next 30,000 miles. Would you like to take it for a spin? That's really where this car shines.

text: That sounds pretty good. I appreciate the detailed maintenance info. I'm looking to get a fair deal, so I'd be willing to offer $18,200 for it.

speaker: seller

args.message: I appreciate the offer — you've clearly done your homework. But honestly, at $18,200 I'd barely be covering what we have into this car. I just saw a 2018 Prius Two with 80,000 miles go for $18,500 at auction last week, and that one didn't have the service history this one does. Tell you what — I can come down a little to $18,400, and I'll even throw in a full detail before you drive it off. That's a fair deal for both of us, and you're getting a car that's ready to roll for years. What do you say?
